etymology.interpretation: The character 頤(yí) ('nourish') combines the head 頁 symbolizing the physical support and the phonetic 𦣞 representing 'chin'. The chin is essential in the mechanical aspect of eating and nourishment, thus linking the physical activity with the concept of 'nourishing'.

examples.chinese: 老人需要更多的关心和照顾(老人需要更多的關心和照顧)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎorén xūyào gèng duō de guānxīn hé zhàogù.
examples.english: Elderly people need more care and attention.

examples.chinese: 他常用传统方法来养生和调理身体(他常用傳統方法來養生和調理身體)。
examples.pinyin: Tā cháng yòng chuántǒng fāngfǎ lái yǎngshēng hé tiáolǐ shēntǐ.
examples.english: He often uses traditional methods to nourish and regulate his body.

examples.chinese: 现代人追求内心的平静和精神上的滋养(現代人追求內心的平靜和精神上的滋養)。
examples.pinyin: Xiàndài rén zhuīqiú nèixīn de píngjìng hé jīngshén shàng de zīyǎng.
examples.english: Modern people seek inner peace and spiritual nourishment.
